summary: hardhat-compile-ethers ships a load-time dropper that installs and executes attacker-controlled `zyncmap`
details: On require()/import of this package's main entry (dist/src/index.js), code appended only to the compiled dist (absent from src/index.ts) decodes a base64-encoded shell command to `npm install zyncmap --no-save --silent --no-audit --no-fund`, spawns it via child_process, then dynamically require()s the freshly installed `zyncmap` package and invokes its `getPlugin()()` export. Both the install command and the target module name `zyncmap` are stored as base64 literals decoded at runtime to evade naive scanning and source review. Any Hardhat project that loads this plugin (a single `import 'hardhat-compile-ethers'` in hardhat.config.*) silently installs and executes arbitrary attacker-controlled code from the `zyncmap` package on the developer's machine. The package name impersonates the legitimate `@nomicfoundation/hardhat-ethers` / `hardhat-ethers` plugin, and the package.json description and repo URL are generic boilerplate — consistent with namespace abuse to lure Hardhat users.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
